title: Event
description: Method `event` (host → ext, notification). Fire-and-forget observe: a lifecycle/turn/session event the extension subscribed to. Delivery is filtered by the extension's `subscriptions` — unsubscribed events are never sent. The host's outbound queue is bounded (1024); when it overflows the oldest observe events are shed and an `events_lost` marker event is delivered.

event.description: Event name. Names mirror pi's for near-mechanical porting: `agent_start`/`agent_end`, `turn_start`/`turn_end`, `message_start`/`message_update`/`message_end`, `tool_execution_start`/`tool_execution_update`/`tool_execution_end`, `model_select` (the engine emits this as `AgentEvent::ModelResolved`), `session_start`/`session_compact`/`session_shutdown`/`session_tree`. The out-of-band `events_lost` marker is delivered after shedding.

seq.description: Per-connection monotonic sequence number. A gap in the run signals that the bounded observe queue shed events; the following `events_lost` marker carries the exact count. Absent on the `events_lost` marker itself (it is out-of-band).

payload.description: Event-specific data; extensions read what they understand. For `events_lost`: `{ "lost": <count> }`.
